response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned polyglot engineer with a strong track record in web, tooling, and infrastructure — but the core requirements of this role are Java, Kotlin, Spring, and deep JVM expertise, none of which feature meaningfully in his background. Java appears once in his skills list and briefly at Altom for robotic arm work; Kotlin is absent entirely. His closest brush with IDE internals is a six-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components for a C IDE, which is architecturally distant from JVM-based IDE development. His primary stack for the last several years has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no demonstrated exposure to enterprise Java frameworks such as Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling experience: built test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators, and agentic developer infrastructure
Brief but real IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments contributing components to NI LabWindows/CVI
Strong software craftsmanship habits (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) that transfer well to any engineering environment
concerns: No Kotlin experience anywhere in the resume — a stated primary requirement for the role
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate exposure; enterprise Java frameworks are entirely absent from his history
Primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang; the JVM is not part of his demonstrated expertise or day-to-day work

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise Java framework experience
